analysis.background: HPS is a rare, high‑fatality zoonotic respiratory disease, and recent travel‑associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have highlighted the need for early radiographic recognition outside endemic areas.
analysis.main_directions: The review focuses on imaging features of HPS, lessons from AI applications in COVID‑19 and viral pneumonia, and practical considerations for developing HPS‑aware AI, including dataset creation, labeling, comparator selection, modeling, validation, and workflow integration.
analysis.current_state: AI tools from the COVID‑19 era are now part of radiology workflows, yet their application to HPS chest imaging remains largely unexplored, and current knowledge is limited to narrative synthesis of imaging findings.
analysis.gaps: Key gaps include data scarcity, low pretest probability in non‑endemic settings, overlapping imaging findings with other pneumonias, and translational challenges from existing AI literature.
analysis.future_research: Future work should involve multi‑institutional data collection, prevalence‑aware evaluation, and multimodal integration of imaging with clinical, laboratory, and epidemiologic data.

analysis.summary_en: Background: HPS is a rare, high‑fatality zoonotic respiratory disease, and recent travel‑associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have highlighted the need for early radiographic recognition outside endemic areas. Main directions: The review focuses on imaging features of HPS, lessons from AI applications in COVID‑19 and viral pneumonia, and practical considerations for developing HPS‑aware AI, including dataset creation, labeling, comparator selection, modeling, validation, and workflow integration. Current state: AI tools from the COVID‑19 era are now part of radiology workflows, yet their application to HPS chest imaging remains largely unexplored, and current knowledge is limited to narrative synthesis of imaging findings. Gaps: Key gaps include data scarcity, low pretest probability in non‑endemic settings, overlapping imaging findings with other pneumonias, and translational challenges from existing AI literature. Future research: Future work should involve multi‑institutional data collection, prevalence‑aware evaluation, and multimodal integration of imaging with clinical, laboratory, and epidemiologic data.

papers.title_zh: 人工智能在汉坦病毒肺综合征胸部影像中的应用:罕见疾病影像的实用考虑综述

analysis_zh.background: HPS 是一种罕见的高死亡率人畜共患呼吸道疾病,最近与旅行相关的安第斯山脉 正汉坦病毒 集群强调了在流行地区之外进行早期放射线识别的必要性。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 该综述重点关注 HPS 的成像特征、AI 在 COVID-19 和病毒性肺炎中应用的经验教训,以及开发 HPS 感知 AI 的实际考虑因素,包括数据集创建、标记、比较器选择、建模、验证和工作流程集成。
analysis_zh.current_state: COVID-19 时代的人工智能工具现已成为放射学工作流程的一部分,但它们在 HPS 胸部成像中的应用在很大程度上仍未得到探索,并且当前的知识仅限于成像结果的叙述性综合。
analysis_zh.gaps: 主要差距包括数据稀缺、非流行环境中预测试概率低、影像学结果与其他肺炎重叠以及现有人工智能文献的转化挑战。
analysis_zh.future_research: 未来的工作应涉及多机构数据收集、患病率评估以及影像与临床、实验室和流行病学数据的多模式整合。

papers.summary_zh: 背景:HPS 是一种罕见的高死亡率人畜共患呼吸道疾病,最近与旅行相关的安第斯山脉 正汉坦病毒 集群强... 主要方向:该综述重点关注 HPS 的成像特征、AI 在 COVID-19 和病毒性肺炎中应用的经验教训,... 研究现状:COVID-19 时代的人工智能工具现已成为放射学工作流程的一部分,但它们在 HPS 胸部成像... 不足:主要差距包括数据稀缺、非流行环境中预测试概率低、影像学结果与其他肺炎重叠以及现有人工智能文献的... 后续研究:未来的工作应涉及多机构数据收集、患病率评估以及影像与临床、实验室和流行病学数据的多模式整合。

papers.title: 2026 Cruise Ship-associated Andes Hantavirus Outbreak and Public Health Response.
papers.abstract: OBJECTIVES: Andes virus (ANDV) is an exceptional hantavirus for which human-to-human transmission has been repeatedly reported, and it causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome with high lethality in South America. This review aims to summarize the epidemiological and clinical features of hantavirus infections and their transmission routes, and to examine transmission patterns within a confined, high-density environment and future public health response strategies, using the 2026 ANDV outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship as a case study. METHODS: Drawing on publicly available reports from the World Health Organization (WHO), the European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control, the Pan American Health Organization, and the Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency, as well as relevant literature, we reviewed the characteristics of hantavirus infections and this outbreak. RESULTS: The MV Hondius departed from Ushuaia, Argentina, on April 1, 2026. On May 2, the UK International Health Regulations (IHR) National Focal Point formally alerted the WHO to a cluster of severe acute respiratory illnesses. By May 27, 13 individuals (11 confirmed and 2 probable cases) had been infected, and 3 had died. Genomic analysis revealed no more than one single-nucleotide polymorphism difference among patients, indicating onboard human-to-human transmission following a single or a very small number of zoonotic spillover events; the effective reproduction number was estimated to be 0.7 on May 22. Major factors contributing to transmission included advanced age (mean, approximately 65 years), shared cabins, communal indoor spaces, and close caregiving or clinical contact. CONCLUSIONS: This outbreak demonstrates that pathogens with limited human-to-human transmissibility can nevertheless be amplified in a closed, high-density environment. Prompt identification, international coordination under the IHR, and efficient risk communication were central to controlling the outbreak, and preparedness for potential importation requires routine assessment of travel history, surveillance for suspected cases, and access to rapid diagnostic testing.

analysis.background: Andes virus (ANDV) is a hantavirus capable of human-to-human transmission and causes severe hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome with high lethality in South America.
analysis.main_directions: The review summarizes epidemiology, clinical features, and transmission routes of hantavirus infections, focusing on transmission patterns in a confined, high-density environment and public health response strategies, illustrated by the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak.
analysis.current_state: In the MV Hondius outbreak, 13 individuals (11 confirmed, 2 probable) were infected and 3 died; genomic analysis showed minimal variation, indicating onboard human-to-human transmission, and the effective reproduction number was 0.7 on May 22.
analysis.gaps: Evidence is limited to publicly available outbreak reports and lacks detailed data on transmission dynamics and risk factors beyond broad observations.
analysis.future_research: Future work should enhance rapid diagnostic testing, routine travel history assessment, surveillance for suspected cases, and coordinated international risk communication to prepare for potential importation of pathogens with limited human-to-human transmissibility.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Andes virus (ANDV) is a hantavirus capable of human-to-human transmission and causes severe hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome with high lethality in South America. Main directions: The review summarizes epidemiology, clinical features, and transmission routes of hantavirus infections, focusing on transmission patterns in a confined, high-density environment and public health response strategies, illustrated by the 2026 MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak. Current state: In the MV Hondius outbreak, 13 individuals (11 confirmed, 2 probable) were infected and 3 died; genomic analysis showed minimal variation, indicating onboard human-to-human transmission, and the effective reproduction number was 0.7 on May 22. Gaps: Evidence is limited to publicly available outbreak reports and lacks detailed data on transmission dynamics and risk factors beyond broad observations. Future research: Future work should enhance rapid diagnostic testing, routine travel history assessment, surveillance for suspected cases, and coordinated international risk communication to prepare for potential importation of pathogens with limited human-to-human transmissibility.

papers.title_zh: 2026年邮轮相关安第斯病毒汉坦病毒爆发与公共卫生应对

analysis_zh.background: 安第斯病毒 (ANDV) 是一种能够在人与人之间传播的 汉坦病毒,并在南美洲造成严重的汉坦病毒心肺综合征,致死率很高。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 该综述总结了 汉坦病毒 感染的流行病学、临床特征和传播途径,重点关注密闭、高密度环境中的传播模式和公共卫生应对策略,以 2026 年 MV Hondius 游轮爆发为例。
analysis_zh.current_state: 在 MV Hondius 疫情中,有 13 人(11 人确诊,2 人疑似)感染,3 人死亡;基因组分析显示变异极小,表明船上存在人际传播,5月22日有效繁殖数为0.7。
analysis_zh.gaps: 证据仅限于公开的疫情报告,缺乏广泛观察之外的传播动态和风险因素的详细数据。
analysis_zh.future_research: 未来的工作应加强快速诊断检测、常规旅行史评估、疑似病例监测以及协调国际风险沟通,为人际传播能力有限的病原体的潜在输入做好准备。

papers.summary_zh: 背景:安第斯病毒 (ANDV) 是一种能够在人与人之间传播的 汉坦病毒,并在南美洲造成严重的汉坦病毒... 主要方向:该综述总结了 汉坦病毒 感染的流行病学、临床特征和传播途径,重点关注密闭、高密度环境中的传播模... 研究现状:在 MV Hondius 疫情中,有 13 人(11 人确诊,2 人疑似)感染,3 人死亡;基... 不足:证据仅限于公开的疫情报告,缺乏广泛观察之外的传播动态和风险因素的详细数据。 后续研究:未来的工作应加强快速诊断检测、常规旅行史评估、疑似病例监测以及协调国际风险沟通,为人际传播能力...

papers.title: Spectrum of Gastrointestinal, Hepatobiliary, and Pancreatic Involvement in Hantavirus Infection: A Narrative Review.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus infection is a zoonotic, rodent-transmitted viral disease that presents with two distinct clinical syndromes: hemorrhagic fever with renal failure (HFRS) and hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS). The 2026 cruise ship hantavirus outbreak has once again brought this infrequent disease into the limelight. Gastrointestinal (GI), hepatobiliary, and pancreatic manifestations, though frequent, have garnered less attention in patients with hantavirus infections. This narrative review summarizes the available literature on GI and hepatopancreatic involvement in hantavirus infection. A comprehensive review of the literature published between 2000 and May 2026 was conducted using PubMed. Eligible studies included observational studies, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series, and case reports describing GI, hepatic, biliary, or pancreatic manifestations in human hantavirus infection. The literature review identified GI symptoms among the earliest and most frequent prodromal manifestations of hantavirus disease. Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and diarrhea often precede organ-specific complications by several days. GI bleeding develops as a component of hemorrhagic syndrome. Hepatic involvement most frequently manifests as transient elevations in aminotransferase levels and, less frequently, as hyperbilirubinemia. Gallbladder wall thickening, acalculous cholecystitis, instances of hantavirus-triggered autoimmune hepatitis, and acute pancreatitis have also been reported. Current management strategies are primarily supportive, including renal replacement therapy for severe renal injury and ventilatory support and extracorporeal membrane oxygenation for severe pulmonary disease. Antiviral therapies such as ribavirin and favipiravir have shown inconsistent or limited clinical benefits. Limited evidence links early and prominent GI symptoms and transaminitis with more severe disease and a worse prognosis. A high index of suspicion in patients with GI symptoms may facilitate earlier diagnosis and allow risk stratification. However, the current evidence is sparse, mostly derived from case reports, case series, and small retrospective studies, which limits our understanding of GI and hepatobiliary-pancreatic manifestations in patients with hantavirus infections. Large prospective studies are needed to clarify the mechanisms, incidence, and prognostic significance of these manifestations.

source_records.title: Spectrum of Gastrointestinal, Hepatobiliary, and Pancreatic Involvement in Hantavirus Infection: A Narrative Review.
source_records.abstract: Hantavirus infection is a zoonotic, rodent-transmitted viral disease that presents with two distinct clinical syndromes: hemorrhagic fever with renal failure (HFRS) and hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS). The 2026 cruise ship hantavirus outbreak has once again brought this infrequent disease into the limelight. Gastrointestinal (GI), hepatobiliary, and pancreatic manifestations, though frequent, have garnered less attention in patients with hantavirus infections. This narrative review summarizes the available literature on GI and hepatopancreatic involvement in hantavirus infection. A comprehensive review of the literature published between 2000 and May 2026 was conducted using PubMed. Eligible studies included observational studies, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series, and case reports describing GI, hepatic, biliary, or pancreatic manifestations in human hantavirus infection. The literature review identified GI symptoms among the earliest and most frequent prodromal manifestations of hantavirus disease. Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and diarrhea often precede organ-specific complications by several days. GI bleeding develops as a component of hemorrhagic syndrome. Hepatic involvement most frequently manifests as transient elevations in aminotransferase levels and, less frequently, as hyperbilirubinemia. Gallbladder wall thickening, acalculous cholecystitis, instances of hantavirus-triggered autoimmune hepatitis, and acute pancreatitis have also been reported. Current management strategies are primarily supportive, including renal replacement therapy for severe renal injury and ventilatory support and extracorporeal membrane oxygenation for severe pulmonary disease. Antiviral therapies such as ribavirin and favipiravir have shown inconsistent or limited clinical benefits. Limited evidence links early and prominent GI symptoms and transaminitis with more severe disease and a worse prognosis. A high index of suspicion in patients with GI symptoms may facilitate earlier diagnosis and allow risk stratification. However, the current evidence is sparse, mostly derived from case reports, case series, and small retrospective studies, which limits our understanding of GI and hepatobiliary-pancreatic manifestations in patients with hantavirus infections. Large prospective studies are needed to clarify the mechanisms, incidence, and prognostic significance of these manifestations.

analysis.background: Hantavirus is a rodent‑borne zoonosis causing hemorrhagic fever with renal failure or pulmonary syndrome, and a recent cruise‑ship outbreak has highlighted its clinical spectrum.
analysis.main_directions: GI, hepatobiliary, and pancreatic involvement are frequent early prodromes, including nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, diarrhea, GI bleeding, transaminitis, gallbladder wall thickening, cholecystitis, autoimmune hepatitis, and pancreatitis; management is mainly supportive with limited antiviral benefit.
analysis.current_state: The narrative review synthesizes literature from 2000‑2026, noting early GI symptoms precede organ injury and that most data come from case reports and small studies.
analysis.gaps: Evidence is sparse and heterogeneous, with inconsistent links between GI/hepatic findings and severity, and a lack of prospective data limits understanding of mechanisms and prognostic value.
analysis.future_research: Large prospective studies are needed to define incidence, mechanisms, and prognostic significance of GI and hepatopancreatic manifestations in hantavirus infection.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantavirus is a rodent‑borne zoonosis causing hemorrhagic fever with renal failure or pulmonary syndrome, and a recent cruise‑ship outbreak has highlighted its clinical spectrum. Main directions: GI, hepatobiliary, and pancreatic involvement are frequent early prodromes, including nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, diarrhea, GI bleeding, transaminitis, gallbladder wall thickening, cholecystitis, autoimmune hepatitis, and pancreatitis; management is mainly supportive with limited antiviral benefit. Current state: The narrative review synthesizes literature from 2000‑2026, noting early GI symptoms precede organ injury and that most data come from case reports and small studies. Gaps: Evidence is sparse and heterogeneous, with inconsistent links between GI/hepatic findings and severity, and a lack of prospective data limits understanding of mechanisms and prognostic value. Future research: Large prospective studies are needed to define incidence, mechanisms, and prognostic significance of GI and hepatopancreatic manifestations in hantavirus infection.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒感染的胃肠、肝胆及胰腺受累范围:叙述性综述

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒 是一种啮齿类动物传播的人畜共患疾病,可导致出血热并伴有肾功能衰竭或肺部综合征,最近的一次游轮疫情凸显了其临床谱。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 胃肠道、肝胆和胰腺受累是常见的早期前驱症状,包括恶心、呕吐、腹痛、腹泻、胃肠道出血、转氨炎、胆囊壁增厚、胆囊炎、自身免疫性肝炎和胰腺炎;管理主要是支持性的,抗病毒效果有限。
analysis_zh.current_state: 叙述性综述综合了 2000 年至 2026 年的文献,指出早期胃肠道症状先于器官损伤,并且大多数数据来自病例报告和小型研究。
analysis_zh.gaps: 证据稀疏且异质,胃肠道/肝脏检查结果与严重程度之间的联系不一致,并且缺乏前瞻性数据限制了对机制和预后价值的理解。
analysis_zh.future_research: 需要进行大型前瞻性研究来确定 汉坦病毒 感染中胃肠道和肝胰腺表现的发生率、机制和预后意义。

papers.summary_zh: 背景:汉坦病毒 是一种啮齿类动物传播的人畜共患疾病,可导致出血热并伴有肾功能衰竭或肺部综合征,最近的... 主要方向:胃肠道、肝胆和胰腺受累是常见的早期前驱症状,包括恶心、呕吐、腹痛、腹泻、胃肠道出血、转氨炎、胆... 研究现状:叙述性综述综合了 2000 年至 2026 年的文献,指出早期胃肠道症状先于器官损伤,并且大多... 不足:证据稀疏且异质,胃肠道/肝脏检查结果与严重程度之间的联系不一致,并且缺乏前瞻性数据限制了对机制... 后续研究:需要进行大型前瞻性研究来确定 汉坦病毒 感染中胃肠道和肝胰腺表现的发生率、机制和预后意义。
